skill.name: backend-api
skill.description: Guides REST API endpoint design, route implementation, HTTP method selection, status code usage, query handling, middleware planning, and API documentation.
skill.summary: Design consistent REST API endpoints and backend route behavior.
skill.author: EIS-ITS

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported path traversal and weak cryptographic algorithm patterns in SKILL.md. Review found no executable code, no cryptographic operations, and no malicious intent. The only confirmed concern is a relative documentation link that points outside the skill folder.

low_findings.title: Out-of-package documentation reference
low_findings.description: SKILL.md links to ../../../agent-os/standards/backend/api.md. This is not executable path traversal, but it may cause the assistant to depend on a file outside the packaged skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The relative path is directly present in the skill file, but it appears to be a documentation reference. There is no evidence of file deletion, secret access, or code execution.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptography pattern false positive
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptographic algorithm terms on lines 3, 13, and 23. Review found normal REST API wording and no cryptographic implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain descriptive REST API guidance only. I found no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, or other weak cryptographic use.

content.value_statement: Backend teams need consistent API routes, methods, responses, and documentation. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through REST endpoint design and implementation.
actual_capabilities: Plans resource-based URL structures for REST APIs.
Maps endpoint behavior to standard HTTP methods.
Guides status code, response, and query parameter choices.
Supports API middleware planning for validation and rate limiting.
Helps draft API documentation and OpenAPI specifications.
limitations: Does not provide framework-specific code by itself.
Depends on project context for exact routing conventions.
Does not validate live API behavior or run tests.
References an external standards file that may not be packaged.

use_cases.title: Create a New Endpoint Set
use_cases.description: Design routes, methods, request rules, and response behavior for a new backend resource.

use_cases.title: Review API Consistency
use_cases.description: Check existing controllers and route definitions against REST naming and status code practices.

use_cases.title: Prepare API Documentation
use_cases.description: Outline endpoint behavior, query parameters, pagination, and error responses for documentation.

prompt_templates.title: Design Basic Routes
prompt_templates.prompt: Design REST endpoints for a new resource. Include URL paths, HTTP methods, success responses, and common error cases.

prompt_templates.title: Add Query Behavior
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me add filtering, sorting, and pagination to this API resource while keeping URL and response conventions consistent.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor Existing Controllers
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these API routes and controllers for REST consistency, status code usage, route naming, and response shape problems.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Versioned API Surface
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a versioned REST API strategy with nested resources, middleware concerns, rate limit headers, and documentation requirements.

output_examples.input: Design endpoints for managing customer orders.
output_examples.output: A route plan with resource URLs, method choices, request validation notes, response summaries, and error handling guidance.

output_examples.input: Review my pagination and filtering approach.
output_examples.output: A consistency review covering query parameter names, default limits, sorting behavior, status codes, and documentation gaps.

output_examples.input: Prepare API documentation for a new resource.
output_examples.output: A documentation outline with endpoint purposes, request fields, response descriptions, authentication notes, and rate limit details.

best_practices: Provide existing route conventions before asking for new endpoint designs.
Include authentication, authorization, and validation requirements early.
Ask for documentation updates when endpoint behavior changes.
anti_patterns: Do not ask it to invent business rules without domain context.
Do not skip project-specific naming and versioning conventions.
Do not treat generated guidance as tested API behavior.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps design and review REST API routes, methods, responses, middleware, and documentation.

faq.question: Can it generate framework-specific code?
faq.answer: It can guide implementation when project context is provided, but the skill itself is framework-neutral.

faq.question: Does it support OpenAPI documentation?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for API documentation and OpenAPI or Swagger specifications.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. Review found low risk and no evidence of malicious behavior.

faq.question: Why is there a filesystem risk factor?
faq.answer: The skill links to a relative standards file outside the skill folder.

faq.question: What should users provide for best results?
faq.answer: Provide current routes, framework details, resource names, authentication rules, and response conventions.
